skill.name: irb-application-assistant
skill.description: Assists researchers with Institutional Review Board (IRB) application tasks, including drafting informed consent documents, reviewing research protocols for compliance, generating application forms, and preparing submission checklists. Use when the user mentions IRB, Institutional Review Board, research ethics, human subjects research, protocol review, informed consent, or needs help preparing or reviewing an IRB application or submission.
skill.summary: Helps researchers draft, review, and validate IRB applications with compliance checks, consent templates, and submission checklists.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 62 potential issues including weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, backtick execution, file writes, and hardcoded URLs. After manual evaluation, 61 of 62 findings are false positives: no cryptographic code exists in any file (false pattern match on substrings like 'des'), 43 backtick detections are Markdown inline code formatting not shell execution, both reconnaissance and hardcoded URL flags are benign documentation text. One legitimate file write exists at scripts/main.py:165 for user-directed output generation, which is low-risk document creation behavior. The static analyzer's 100/100 risk score is entirely based on false positives and does not reflect actual risk.

low_findings.title: File write to user-specified output path
low_findings.description: Script writes generated IRB application content to a file path specified via --output argument. While this is expected document-generation behavior with a safe default path (irb_application.txt), an attacker with local access could specify arbitrary paths. Risk is low because the tool runs locally and the user controls the output destination.

dangerous_patterns.title: File write operation in script
dangerous_patterns.description: Python open() with write mode used at scripts/main.py:165 to save generated IRB documents to a user-specified output file. This is legitimate document-generation functionality with a safe default path. No exfiltration or sensitive path writing detected.

content.user_title: Generate IRB applications, consent forms, and compliance reports
content.value_statement: Preparing IRB applications for human subjects research is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill helps researchers draft compliant consent forms, review protocols, generate application documents, and validate submission packages using structured workflows with explicit assumptions and fallback paths.
actual_capabilities: Generate IRB application sections including protocol summary, study procedures, and risk assessment
Produce informed consent form templates with standard required sections
Provide structured workflow guidance for IRB submission preparation
Deliver reproducible outputs with explicit assumptions and documented fallback paths
Support multiple task types via command-line interface with configurable parameters
limitations: Does not submit applications directly to any IRB system or platform
Cannot guarantee regulatory approval since requirements vary by institution and jurisdiction
Outputs are templates that require researcher review and customization for specific studies
Script uses placeholder text for fields that need real study data to be meaningful

use_cases.title: First-time IRB submission for graduate researchers
use_cases.description: A graduate student preparing their first IRB application uses the skill to generate properly structured consent forms and compliance checklists, reducing the risk of submission rejection due to formatting errors or missing sections.

use_cases.title: Protocol compliance review for research coordinators
use_cases.description: A research coordinator managing multiple studies uses the skill to run compliance checks across protocols, flagging missing sections and non-compliant elements before formal IRB submission.

use_cases.title: Submission package validation for principal investigators
use_cases.description: A principal investigator uses the skill to validate complete IRB submission packages, ensuring all required documents are present and all fields are complete before the final review and sign-off.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an informed consent template
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an informed consent form for a minimal risk study with adult participants. The study involves surveys and interviews about healthcare experiences.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic consent form generation

prompt_templates.title: Run a protocol compliance check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my research protocol for IRB compliance and flag any missing sections or issues. The protocol is a survey-based study with anonymized data collection.

prompt_templates.title: Generate full IRB application package
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete IRB initial review application for my study. I need the protocol summary, risk assessment, privacy protection plan, and consent process documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Validate submission and resolve issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate my IRB submission package and create a prioritized list of issues to fix. For each issue, explain what is missing and how to resolve it before resubmission.

output_examples.input: Generate an informed consent form for a minimal risk study with adult participants about healthcare experiences
output_examples.output: A structured consent form document with sections for study purpose, procedures, risks and discomforts, benefits, and confidentiality. Each section contains appropriate template language for minimal risk research with adult populations.

output_examples.input: Run a compliance check on my protocol for a survey-based study with anonymized data
output_examples.output: A compliance report listing each required protocol section with pass or flag status. Flagged items include missing data management plan and incomplete risk mitigation description, with specific recommendations for each gap.

best_practices: Always confirm the study scope, population, and risk level with the researcher before generating documents
Review all generated templates against the specific IRB requirements of the target institution
Use the recommended workflow order: compliance check first, then generate documents, then validate the package
anti_patterns: Do not submit generated documents directly to an IRB without thorough human review and customization
Do not assume generated templates work for all institutions without checking local IRB requirements and forms
Do not skip the compliance check step and proceed directly to document generation for complex protocols

faq.question: What IRB-related tasks can this skill help with?
faq.answer: This skill helps draft informed consent documents, review research protocols for compliance, generate IRB application forms, and validate submission packages for completeness. It provides structured workflows with explicit assumptions and documented fallback paths.

faq.question: Does this skill submit applications directly to an IRB?
faq.answer: No, this skill does not submit applications to any IRB system. It generates documents, reports, and checklists that researchers must review, customize, and submit through their institution's official IRB submission process.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee IRB approval?
faq.answer: No, this skill cannot guarantee IRB approval. IRB requirements vary by institution, jurisdiction, and study type. Generated documents are starting templates that need researcher review and institution-specific customization.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need to provide study details including title, principal investigator, target population, study procedures, risk level, and data management plans. The skill works best when you have completed your study design before generating documents.

faq.question: What output formats does this skill produce?
faq.answer: The Python script produces plain text output by default. The skill workflows can guide document creation in various formats including DOCX, TXT, and PDF depending on the task parameters and your environment configuration.

faq.question: Can this skill handle multi-site or international research protocols?
faq.answer: The skill provides general IRB application templates based on standard Common Rule requirements. For multi-site or international research, you should supplement generated documents with site-specific requirements and consult local IRB offices for jurisdiction-specific guidance.
